title: Minimum standard VAT rate
rule: EU member states must apply a standard VAT rate of at least 15%.
details: Article 97 of the VAT Directive (2006/112/EC) sets the minimum standard rate. Member states set their own rate at or above this floor.
exceptions: Some member states have transitional derogations for lower standard rates

title: Reduced VAT rates
rule: Member states may apply one or two reduced rates of not less than 5% to goods and services listed in Annex III.
details: Reduced rates apply to items like food, water, pharmaceuticals, books, accommodation, and cultural events. The exact items vary by member state.
exceptions: Super-reduced rates (<5%) grandfathered for some member states
Zero rates permitted where they existed before 1991
source: Council Directive 2006/112/EC, Articles 98-99 and Annex III

title: B2C digital services — place of supply
rule: For B2C supplies of electronically supplied services, the place of supply is where the customer is established or has their usual residence.
details: Since 2015, digital services (SaaS, streaming, downloads, e-books) are taxed where the customer is, not where the supplier is. This applies to telecommunications, broadcasting, and electronically supplied services.
exceptions: Small businesses under EUR 10,000 threshold can use their home country rate (since 2019)

title: B2B services — general rule
rule: For B2B supplies of services, the place of supply is where the business customer is established.
details: The general rule for B2B services is that VAT is charged where the customer's business is established. The customer accounts for VAT via the reverse charge mechanism.
exceptions: Specific rules for immovable property, transport, cultural events, restaurant services

title: Reverse charge mechanism
rule: For B2B cross-border services within the EU, the customer is liable to account for VAT (reverse charge), not the supplier.
details: The supplier issues an invoice without VAT. The customer self-assesses the VAT due in their country and includes it on their VAT return (both as output and input VAT, resulting in net zero effect for fully-taxable businesses).
exceptions: Does not apply to B2C transactions
Some goods subject to domestic reverse charge (construction, waste, electronics in some MS)
formula: Invoice amount (excl. VAT) × customer's local VAT rate = VAT to self-assess

title: OSS — One Stop Shop
rule: The One Stop Shop (OSS) allows businesses to register in a single EU member state to declare and pay VAT on cross-border B2C sales of goods and services across the EU.
details: Instead of registering for VAT in every EU country where they make B2C sales, businesses can use OSS to file a single quarterly return covering all EU member states. There are three schemes: Union OSS (EU-based sellers), Non-Union OSS (non-EU sellers of services), and IOSS (import scheme for goods up to EUR 150).
exceptions: Mandatory VAT registration in the customer's country still required if the business has a fixed establishment there
source: Council Directive 2006/112/EC, Articles 369a-369k (as amended by Directive 2017/2455)

title: EUR 10,000 threshold for micro-businesses
rule: Businesses with total cross-border B2C digital service sales under EUR 10,000 per year may charge VAT at their home country rate instead of the customer's country rate.
details: This simplification was introduced in 2019 to reduce the compliance burden on micro-businesses. Once the EUR 10,000 threshold is exceeded, the business must charge the customer's local rate for all subsequent sales in that calendar year.
exceptions: Threshold is cumulative across all EU member states
Once exceeded, applies for remainder of year plus the following year

title: Mandatory invoice content
rule: VAT invoices must contain: date of issue, sequential number, supplier and customer VAT identification numbers, full names and addresses, description of goods/services, quantity, unit price excl. VAT, VAT rate, VAT amount, and total amount.
details: Article 226 lists the mandatory elements. Electronic invoices are equivalent to paper invoices if they ensure authenticity of origin and integrity of content. Simplified invoices (under EUR 100) require fewer elements.
exceptions: Simplified invoices allowed for amounts under EUR 100 (or MS-specific higher threshold)
Some member states have additional requirements

title: VAT calculation — tax-exclusive pricing
rule: VAT is calculated on the net price (tax-exclusive). Total = Net + (Net × VAT rate).
details: In the EU, prices displayed to consumers must include VAT (tax-inclusive). For B2B, prices are typically quoted tax-exclusive. When extracting VAT from a gross price: VAT = Gross - (Gross / (1 + rate)).
exceptions: Some member states allow tax-exclusive pricing in specific B2B contexts
formula: VAT_amount = net_price × vat_rate; gross_price = net_price + VAT_amount; To extract: VAT_amount = gross_price - (gross_price / (1 + vat_rate))

title: Financial and insurance services exemption
rule: Financial services (banking, credit, securities transactions) and insurance services are exempt from VAT without the right to deduct input VAT.
details: This exemption covers lending, credit guarantees, deposit taking, payment transactions, currency exchange, and securities trading. Insurance and reinsurance transactions including related services are also exempt.
exceptions: Some member states allow optional taxation for financial services to taxable persons
Safe custody and management of securities may be taxable in some MS

title: Education and training exemption
rule: Education and vocational training provided by public bodies or recognized organizations is exempt from VAT.
details: This covers school and university education, vocational training, and closely related services. Private tuition by teachers in school and university subjects is also exempt.
exceptions: Commercial training by non-recognized bodies is generally taxable
Some member states interpret 'recognized organization' narrowly

title: Healthcare exemption
rule: Medical and hospital care provided by public bodies or under comparable conditions is exempt from VAT.
details: Covers hospital and medical care, closely related activities, supply of human organs, blood, and milk. Also covers services by medical and paramedical practitioners.
exceptions: Cosmetic surgery is generally taxable
Some member states tax certain paramedical services

title: Luxembourg VAT rates
rule: Luxembourg applies: standard rate 17%, intermediate rate 14%, reduced rate 8%, super-reduced rate 3%.
details: Luxembourg has the lowest standard VAT rate in the EU at 17%. The super-reduced rate of 3% applies to food, books, children's clothing, and certain other goods. The 8% rate applies to certain services. The 14% rate applies to specific goods and services.
exceptions: Rates were temporarily reduced by 1pp in 2023-2024 (returned to normal rates in 2025)
source: Luxembourg VAT Law (Loi modifiée du 12 février 1979)

title: Germany VAT rates
rule: Germany applies: standard rate 19%, reduced rate 7%.
details: The reduced rate of 7% applies to food, books, newspapers, public transport, hotel accommodation, and cultural events. Restaurant and catering services are taxed at 19% (the temporary 7% rate during COVID-19 ended).
exceptions: Agricultural producers may use a flat-rate scheme

title: France VAT rates
rule: France applies: standard rate 20%, intermediate rate 10%, reduced rate 5.5%, super-reduced rate 2.1%.
details: The 10% rate applies to restaurant meals, transport, renovations. The 5.5% rate applies to food, books, energy. The 2.1% rate applies to pharmaceuticals and press publications.
exceptions: Corsica and overseas territories have special rates
Press publications eligible for super-reduced 2.1%
source: Code général des impôts, Article 278 et seq.

title: Belgium VAT rates
rule: Belgium applies: standard rate 21%, intermediate rate 12%, reduced rate 6%.
details: The 6% rate applies to basic necessities, water supply, renovation of private dwellings. The 12% rate applies to restaurant services, social housing, and certain energy products.
exceptions: Special scheme for small enterprises under EUR 25,000 turnover

title: Netherlands VAT rates
rule: Netherlands applies: standard rate 21%, reduced rate 9%.
details: The reduced rate of 9% applies to food, water, pharmaceuticals, books, hotel accommodation, hairdressing, and public transport.
exceptions: Zero rate applies to intra-community supplies and exports

title: Italy VAT rates
rule: Italy applies: standard rate 22%, reduced rate 10%, super-reduced rates 5% and 4%.
details: The 4% rate applies to basic food, books, newspapers. The 5% rate applies to some food and health products. The 10% rate applies to tourism, cultural events, some energy.
exceptions: Agricultural flat-rate scheme available

title: Spain VAT rates
rule: Spain applies: standard rate 21%, reduced rate 10%, super-reduced rate 4%.
details: The 4% rate applies to bread, milk, eggs, fruit, vegetables, books, pharmaceuticals, wheelchairs. The 10% rate applies to food, water, transport, hotel accommodation.
exceptions: Canary Islands have IGIC instead of VAT
Ceuta and Melilla have IPSI

title: Portugal VAT rates
rule: Portugal applies: standard rate 23%, intermediate rate 13%, reduced rate 6%.
details: The 6% rate applies to basic food, books, pharmaceuticals, hotel accommodation. The 13% rate applies to certain food and beverages, agricultural inputs.
exceptions: Azores and Madeira have lower rates (standard 16%/22%)

title: Austria VAT rates
rule: Austria applies: standard rate 20%, reduced rates 13% and 10%.
details: The 10% rate applies to food, books, pharmaceuticals, public transport. The 13% rate applies to plants, cultural events, accommodation.
exceptions: Agriculture flat-rate scheme available

title: Ireland VAT rates
rule: Ireland applies: standard rate 23%, reduced rates 13.5% and 9%, zero rate 0%.
details: Ireland is one of few EU states with a zero VAT rate (on food, children's clothing, books, oral medicines). The 9% rate applies to newspapers, electronic publications. The 13.5% rate applies to fuel, electricity, building services.
exceptions: Zero rating is a historical derogation from EU VAT Directive

title: Poland VAT rates
rule: Poland applies: standard rate 23%, reduced rates 8% and 5%.
details: The 5% rate applies to basic food, books. The 8% rate applies to certain food, construction services, health-related goods.
exceptions: Temporary rate reductions may apply during economic measures
source: Ustawa o podatku od towarów i usług, Article 41

title: Sweden VAT rates
rule: Sweden applies: standard rate 25%, reduced rates 12% and 6%.
details: The 12% rate applies to food, hotel accommodation, restaurant services. The 6% rate applies to books, newspapers, cultural events, passenger transport.
